title: agent-runtime: scheduling gaps - cron, event triggers, rate limiting
summary: Issue #592 tracks daemon trigger hardening for cron semantics, external event sources, admission control/rate limiting/backpressure, deduplication, and failed-trigger retry/DLQ handling. Current staging already has a durable trigger queue in agent-runtime, so implementation should extend that work and reconcile agent-platform parity rather than creating a competing queue.

triageFindings: The issue reports cron limitations: no named months/days, timezone support, L/# syntax, or @reboot/@daily-style macros.
The issue reports missing event triggers beyond file/webhook/timer, especially message queues, git webhooks, and Slack/Discord-style chat triggers.
The issue reports no trigger rate limiting, backpressure, or max-per-minute protection against runaway webhook senders.
The issue reports no cross-rule trigger deduplication; overlapping file patterns can fire multiple times.
The issue reports no dead letter queue, retry metadata, backoff, or failure metrics.
Current staging has packages/agent-runtime/src/daemon/durableQueue.ts with persisted retry/dead-letter behavior, while packages/agent-platform/src/daemon lacks that parity.

relatedIssues.reason: Crash recovery and trigger replay. Reuse durable queue primitives and avoid inventing a separate persistence model.
Background process lifecycle and backpressure. Keep this issue focused on trigger admission/scheduling, not process lifecycle.

existingPlanPr.note: A prior plan PR/comment existed for this issue, but the current requested branch/artifacts were absent locally; this plan refreshes against current staging code.

methodologyReferencesToPreserve: parity review for duplicated package implementations

knownCurrentState.agentRuntimeQueue: packages/agent-runtime/src/daemon/durableQueue.ts persists trigger-queue.json records, replays pending events after restart, retries failed events with backoff, and moves exhausted records to dead-letter.
knownCurrentState.agentRuntimeLoop: packages/agent-runtime/src/daemon/loop.ts can use DurableTriggerQueue when logDir is present, writes daemon.status.json with active/pending/deadLetter counts, and logs TRIGGER_FAILED/QUEUE_ERROR events.
knownCurrentState.agentPlatformDaemon: packages/agent-platform/src/daemon duplicates the older daemon loop and scheduler code without DurableTriggerQueue or deadLetterRuns status.
knownCurrentState.cron: timerScheduler.ts parses exactly five fields with numeric values, commas, ranges, and steps, then matches using local Date getters.
knownCurrentState.fileWatcher: fileWatcher.ts creates one watcher/debounce timer per file trigger and does not deduplicate the same changed path across overlapping rules.
knownCurrentState.webhook: webhookListener.ts supports method/path/bearer auth, 1 MiB body limit, JSON parsing, and immediate 200 ok after enqueueing callback. It has no admission/rate-limit response contract.
knownCurrentState.externalTriggerConcepts: packages/triggers-mux normalizes CI/webhook events for agent launching, and packages/krate/core models AgentTriggerRule/AgentTriggerExecution with basic cron/webhook/comment/label concepts.

reuseAuditSeed.newInfrastructureConstraint: Do not create a second trigger persistence or DLQ subsystem. Extend DurableTriggerQueue and reconcile package parity before adding new storage or broker abstractions.

acceptanceFocus: Phase 0 reuse-audit findings are rendered before proposing new trigger infrastructure.
Existing file/webhook/timer trigger configs and existing five-field numeric cron expressions remain compatible.
Cron support is explicitly specified and tested for named months/days, timezone semantics, macros, and any unsupported syntax that remains out of scope.
Event-source abstraction has a narrow, documented interface and does not require live RabbitMQ, Kafka, SQS, Slack, or Discord services for unit tests.
Webhook and loop admission control expose deterministic accepted/deferred/rejected behavior, bounded queue depth, rate-limit policy, and observable status/log output.
Deduplication has explicit fingerprints, TTL/window semantics, and logs/metrics for suppressed duplicate triggers without suppressing legitimate distinct events.
Retry and DLQ behavior reuse DurableTriggerQueue, include backoff and failure metadata, and coordinate with #585 rather than replacing it.
agent-runtime and agent-platform daemon implementations are centralized or kept in tested parity for every implemented trigger behavior.
Security review confirms webhook auth, payload/log redaction, DLQ redaction, and denial-of-service behavior.
Docs and docs/agent-layer-gaps.md are updated only for gaps actually closed.
nonGoals: Do not implement full daemon crash recovery, watchdog restart, or supervisor/systemd integration beyond reusing DurableTriggerQueue boundaries from #585.
Do not implement background process lifecycle management, pause/resume, process dependency ordering, or stream backpressure from #593.
Do not require live external brokers, cloud services, Slack, Discord, Docker, Kubernetes, or network dependencies for core verification.
Do not silently change existing webhook success responses without an intentional compatibility plan.
Do not introduce a second durable queue, DLQ, or retry persistence model.
Do not generate shell-oriented process subtasks for this repository workflow.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #592, comments, labels, docs/agent-layer-gaps.md, and related #585/#593 boundaries to lock source-of-truth scope.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Search existing daemon queue, trigger, observability, external trigger, and duplicate agent-platform infrastructure before proposing anything new.

plannedPhases.name: Trigger architecture trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Trace config validation, timer scheduler, file watcher, webhook listener, runDaemonLoop, DurableTriggerQueue, diagnostics, tests, and platform duplication.

plannedPhases.name: Capability spec
plannedPhases.purpose: Define staged cron, event-source, admission, deduplication, retry/DLQ, and parity semantics before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Regression coverage
plannedPhases.purpose: Add focused failing tests for cron semantics, event-source abstraction, admission control, deduplication, retry/DLQ, and runtime/platform parity.

plannedPhases.name: Design review
plannedPhases.purpose: Challenge scope, compatibility, DoS behavior, false deduplication risk, DLQ/redaction, and package parity before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Incremental implementation
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement accepted trigger-hardening slices in small steps while preserving existing behavior and reusing DurableTriggerQueue.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted daemon tests, package tests/builds, metadata checks, and iterate up to three attempts when verification or review fails.

plannedPhases.name: Security and compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review public config/API changes, trigger storm behavior, payload redaction, DLQ records, and runtime/platform parity.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare requirements directly to implementation, verification, review, docs, and follow-up boundaries before declaring completion.

breakpoints.when: Only if design discovers a real maintainer decision, such as whether @reboot must run on daemon start, how to handle unsupported L/# cron syntax, whether to change webhook response status for deferred triggers, or whether to centralize daemon code across packages.
Only if final acceptance finds a partially implemented gap that requires explicit deferral or scope approval.

qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse-audit findings are rendered before implementation recommendations.
Capability spec defines accepted scope, explicit non-goals, and compatibility behavior before code changes.
Regression tests are authored before production implementation and the red phase is verified for expected missing behavior.
Cron tests cover existing numeric expressions plus accepted named month/day, macro, timezone, and unsupported syntax behavior.
Admission tests cover accepted, deferred, rate-limited, and queue-full outcomes plus status/log visibility.
Deduplication tests cover overlapping file rules, webhook/timer fingerprints, TTL/window behavior, and false-positive safeguards.
Retry/DLQ tests prove DurableTriggerQueue reuse, backoff, dead-letter state, status counts, and payload/secret redaction.
Parity tests prove agent-runtime and agent-platform share or match daemon trigger behavior.
Targeted daemon tests pass for every affected package.
Package-level agent-runtime and agent-platform tests/builds pass, or unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with exact evidence.
Security review approves webhook auth/backpressure, denial-of-service limits, and DLQ/log payload handling.
Final spec guard confirms no unapproved expansion into #585 crash recovery or #593 background process lifecycle.
